assembly_instructions: Open-source 'skateboard' electric-vehicle chassis — a bolted square-tube steel space frame with a flat battery tray, ready to have any body shell dropped on top. Two long side rails and two cross members frame the floor pan, which carries a flat under-floor Li-ion pack. At each corner: a wheel, a vertical coilover strut, and a lower lateral A-arm connecting the chassis to the wheel hub. Front: a rack-and-pinion steering bar between the wheels, a driver-side steering column, and a front bumper tube. Rear: a transverse electric motor coupled to a reduction gearbox and a transverse drive shaft to the rear wheels. On-floor electronics: motor controller, BMS, DC/DC converter. A side-mounted charge port, two seats, and 2.33 × 1.49 × 1.38 m overall dimensions.

info.japanese_name: TABBY EVO（オープンソース EV プラットフォーム）
info.english_name: Open Motors TABBY EVO — Open-Source Electric Vehicle Platform
info.summary: Italian-designed open-hardware 'skateboard' EV chassis. A modular, rolling platform intended as a starting point for EV startups, OEM concept builds, and university teaching.
info.description: TABBY EVO is the second-generation open-source electric-vehicle platform from OSVehicle / Open Motors. It is a 'skateboard' rolling chassis — a welded/bolted steel space frame with suspension, steering, brakes, battery, motor, and electronics all pre-integrated, ready to take any body shell bolted on top. The architecture follows a rear-drive layout with a single transverse electric motor coupled to a reduction gearbox and a transverse drive shaft to the rear wheels, while the battery pack sits flat under the floor between the front and rear axles. A motor controller, battery management system (BMS), and a DC/DC converter ride on the floor beside the battery; a charge port pierces the side rail. Full CAD, bill of materials, wiring diagrams, and assembly manuals are released under CC BY-SA 4.0 so that others can build, fork, or commercialize the platform.
info.operator: Open Motors community + EV startups using TABBY as a reference chassis
info.contract_value: Reference BOM published; no fixed list price
info.status: Open-source; documents and CAD archived and publicly downloadable

facts.label: Architecture
facts.value: 'Skateboard' floor frame with rear-drive electric powertrain

facts.label: Powertrain
facts.value: Transverse electric motor → reduction gearbox → transverse drive shaft (rear wheels)

facts.label: Electronics
facts.value: Motor controller + DC/DC converter + BMS, floor-mounted

facts.label: Chassis
facts.value: Bolted square-tube steel space frame with corner A-arm suspension

facts.label: License
facts.value: CC BY-SA 4.0 (all CAD, BOM, and docs)

sources.title: Open Motors — TABBY EVO product page

parts.name: Chassis Floor Pan / Battery Tray
parts.role: Central floor pan welded into the chassis rails — carries the flat battery pack underneath.

parts.name: Side Rail (Port)
parts.role: Port-side longitudinal square-tube rail — full 2.33 m chassis length.

parts.name: Side Rail (Starboard)
parts.role: Starboard-side longitudinal square-tube rail.

parts.name: Front Cross Member
parts.role: Forward cross member tying the two side rails together and carrying the steering rack + bumper.

parts.name: Rear Cross Member
parts.role: Rear cross member — the powertrain mounts to it.

parts.name: Wheel (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: Front-left wheel.

parts.name: Wheel (Fwd-Right)
parts.role: Front-right wheel.

parts.name: Wheel (Rear-Left)
parts.role: Rear-left wheel — driven by the transverse drive shaft.

parts.name: Wheel (Rear-Right)
parts.role: Rear-right wheel — driven by the transverse drive shaft.

parts.name: Coilover Strut (FL)
parts.role: Front-left coilover strut — shock absorber + spring on top of the A-arm.

parts.name: Coilover Strut (FR)
parts.role: Front-right coilover strut.

parts.name: Coilover Strut (RL)
parts.role: Rear-left coilover strut.

parts.name: Coilover Strut (RR)
parts.role: Rear-right coilover strut.

parts.name: Lower A-Arm (FL)
parts.role: Front-left lower lateral A-arm between the chassis and the wheel hub.

parts.name: Lower A-Arm (FR)
parts.role: Front-right lower A-arm.

parts.name: Lower A-Arm (RL)
parts.role: Rear-left lower A-arm.

parts.name: Lower A-Arm (RR)
parts.role: Rear-right lower A-arm.

parts.name: Steering Rack
parts.role: Rack-and-pinion steering bar between the front wheels.

parts.name: Steering Column
parts.role: Tilted steering column on the driver side (LHD).

parts.name: Electric Motor
parts.role: Transverse AC traction motor.

parts.name: Reduction Gearbox
parts.role: Single-speed reduction box between motor and drive shaft.

parts.name: Rear Drive Shaft
parts.role: Transverse drive shaft — delivers torque from the gearbox to both rear wheels.

parts.name: Under-Floor Battery Pack
parts.role: Flat Li-ion battery pack under the chassis floor between the axles.

parts.name: Battery Management System
parts.role: BMS — per-cell voltage/temperature monitoring and balancing.

parts.name: Motor Controller / Inverter
parts.role: Three-phase inverter — DC to AC for the traction motor.

parts.name: DC/DC Converter
parts.role: Steps the high-voltage traction pack down to 12 V for the accessory bus.

parts.name: Charge Port
parts.role: Exterior AC/DC charge port on the port side rail.

parts.name: Driver Seat
parts.role: Driver's seat on the port side (LHD).

parts.name: Passenger Seat
parts.role: Front-right passenger seat.

parts.name: Front Bumper
parts.role: Front bumper tube spanning the two side rails.
